examples.title: CDP Instruction Example
examples.description: Example of CDP instruction performing coprocessor operation
examples.code:
; CDP instruction format: CDP p<n>, <opcode1>, Cd, Cn, Cm, <opcode2>
; Example: Initialize floating-point coprocessor
CDP p10, 0, c5, c0, c0, 0 ; Load floating-point control register
CDP p10, 7, c5, c0, c0, 0 ; Enable floating-point unit
examples.explanation: CDP performs data processing within coprocessor registers. Here CP10 (VFP) control register is initialized using CDP instructions with different opcodes.

examples.title: LDC Instruction Example
examples.description: Loading floating-point constant from memory to coprocessor
examples.code:
; Load floating-point value to VFP register
LDC p11, c8, [r0] ; Load from memory at address in R0
 ; to VFP register d8 (c8 = lower 32-bits)
LDC p11, c9, [r0, #4]! ; Pre-indexed: load next value
examples.explanation: LDC transfers data from memory to coprocessor register. Here floating-point values are loaded from memory to VFP (CP11) data registers.

examples.title: MRC/MCR Instruction Example
examples.description: Reading and writing system control coprocessor registers
examples.code:
; Read CP15 control register (MRC)
MRC p15, 0, r0, c1, c0, 0 ; Read CP15 c1 to ARM register r0
 ; c1 = Control Register

; Write to CP15 control register (MCR)
MOV r1, #0x1000
MCR p15, 0, r1, c1, c0, 0 ; Write r1 to CP15 c1
 ; Enable MMU or cache
examples.explanation: MRC reads from coprocessor to ARM register (useful for system status), MCR writes from ARM to coprocessor (useful for system configuration).

examples.title: NEON SIMD Vector Addition
examples.description: Using NEON for parallel addition of 4 floating-point values
examples.code:
; R0 = address of array A, R1 = address of array B, R2 = result
; Assuming 4 float values (16 bytes = 128 bits)
VLD1.32 {d0, d1}, [r0] ; Load 4 floats from array A to Q0
VLD1.32 {d2, d3}, [r1] ; Load 4 floats from array B to Q1
VADD.f32 q2, q0, q1 ; Add Q0 + Q1 → Q2 (4 additions!)
VST1.32 {d4, d5}, [r2] ; Store results to array B
; Instead of 4 scalar ADD operations, we do 1 SIMD operation
examples.explanation: NEON SIMD processes 4 floating-point values in parallel with single instruction. This provides 4x speedup over scalar processing.
